zh.lifeSummary: 當Hester Mercier於1602年8月1日之前出生在英國哈普爾素坦普頓時，她的父親Jean Le Mercier46歲，母親Jane Le Clere44歲。她於大約1620年與Nathaniel Batchiler在英國哈普爾素坦普頓結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1633年在英國英格蘭哈普爾素坦普頓。

nameMeanings.name: Mercier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Gaston, Lucien, Aurele, Jacques, Normand, Laurent, Michel, Valmont.

French and Channel Islands (Jersey): occupational name for a merchant, from Old French mercier, from Late Latin merciarius (an agent derivative of merx, genitive mercis ‘merchandise’). This name was also taken to England by Huguenot refugees. Compare Marshall and Mercy .

variants.name: Mercereau
meanings: French: occupational name from a diminutive of Old French mercier ‘merchant’ (see Mercier ). Compare Mersereau .

History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its altered form Mersereau) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Marshall
meanings: English: usually an occupational name ‘farrier’, occasionally a status name ‘chief official of a royal household or court; a high officer of state’, from Middle English mareshal and Old French maresc(h)al. An even wider range of meanings is found in some other languages: compare for example Polish Marszałek (see Marszalek ). This name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. It is also borne by Jews, presumably as an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Mercy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jean Robert.
French: habitational name from any of the places so named in Allier, Meuthe-et-Moselle, and Yonne, derived from the Latin personal name Marcius, Martius, or Mercius + the locative suffix -acum.

variants.name: Mercer
meanings: English and Catalan: occupational name for a merchant, from Old French mercier, merchier (see Mercier ). In Middle English the term was applied particularly to someone who dealt in textiles, especially the more costly and luxurious fabrics such as silks, satin, and velvet.

nameMeanings.name: Hester
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of Esther , of medieval origin. For a long while the two forms were interchangeable, the addition or dropping of h- being commonplace in a whole range of words, but now they are generally regarded as two distinct names.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Hester Mercier was born before 1 August 1602, in Southampton, Hampshire, England, her father, Jean Le Mercier, was 46 and her mother, Jane Le Clere, was 44. She married Nathaniel Batchiler about 1620, in Southampton, Hampshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1633, in Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Nathaniel Batchiler environ 1620, à Southampton, Hampshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1633, à Southampton, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Hester Mercier(이)가 1602년 8월 1일 이전 영국 함프실르 소우섬프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Jean Le Mercier(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jane Le Clere(은)는 44세였다. 그녀는 대략 1620년 영국 함프실르 소우섬프턴에서 Nathaniel Batchiler(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1633년 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 소우섬프턴에서 사망했다.

Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Nathaniel Batchiler около 1620 г. в местности соутамптон, хампшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1633 г. в местности соутамптон, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания.
